창세기 26:1.content: There was a famine in the land, in addition to the first famine that was in the days of Abraham. Isaac went to Abimelech king of the Philistines, to Gerar.

창세기 26:2.content: Yahweh appeared to him, and said, “Don’t go down into Egypt. Live in the land I will tell you about.

창세기 26:3.content: Live in this land, and I will be with you, and will bless you. For I will give to you, and to your offspring, all these lands, and I will establish the oath which I swore to Abraham your father.

창세기 26:4.content: I will multiply your offspring as the stars of the sky, and will give all these lands to your offspring. In your offspring all the nations of the earth will be blessed,

창세기 26:5.content: because Abraham obeyed my voice, and kept my requirements, my commandments, my statutes, and my laws.”

창세기 26:7.content: The men of the place asked him about his wife. He said, “She is my sister,” for he was afraid to say, “My wife”, lest, he thought, “the men of the place might kill me for Rebekah, because she is beautiful to look at.”

창세기 26:8.content: When he had been there a long time, Abimelech king of the Philistines looked out at a window, and saw, and, behold, Isaac was caressing Rebekah, his wife.

창세기 26:9.content: Abimelech called Isaac, and said, “Behold, surely she is your wife. Why did you say, ‘She is my sister?’ ” Isaac said to him, “Because I said, ‘Lest I die because of her.’ ”

창세기 26:10.content: Abimelech said, “What is this you have done to us? One of the people might easily have lain with your wife, and you would have brought guilt on us!”

창세기 26:11.content: Abimelech commanded all the people, saying, “He who touches this man or his wife will surely be put to death.”

창세기 26:12.content: Isaac sowed in that land, and reaped in the same year one hundred times what he planted. Yahweh blessed him.

창세기 26:13.content: The man grew great, and grew more and more until he became very great.

창세기 26:14.content: He had possessions of flocks, possessions of herds, and a great household. The Philistines envied him.

창세기 26:15.content: Now all the wells which his father’s servants had dug in the days of Abraham his father, the Philistines had stopped, and filled with earth.

창세기 26:16.content: Abimelech said to Isaac, “Go away from us, for you are much mightier than we.”

창세기 26:17.content: Isaac departed from there, encamped in the valley of Gerar, and lived there.

창세기 26:18.content: Isaac dug again the wells of water, which they had dug in the days of Abraham his father, for the Philistines had stopped them after the death of Abraham. He called their names after the names by which his father had called them.

창세기 26:19.content: Isaac’s servants dug in the valley, and found there a well of springing water.

창세기 26:20.content: The herdsmen of Gerar argued with Isaac’s herdsmen, saying, “The water is ours.” He called the name of the well Esek, because they contended with him.

창세기 26:21.content: They dug another well, and they argued over that, also. He called its name Sitnah.

창세기 26:22.content: He left that place, and dug another well. They didn’t argue over that one. He called it Rehoboth. He said, “For now Yahweh has made room for us, and we will be fruitful in the land.”

창세기 26:23.content: He went up from there to Beersheba.

창세기 26:24.content: Yahweh appeared to him the same night, and said, “I am the God of Abraham your father. Don’t be afraid, for I am with you, and will bless you, and multiply your offspring for my servant Abraham’s sake.”

창세기 26:25.content: He built an altar there, and called on Yahweh’s name, and pitched his tent there. There Isaac’s servants dug a well.

창세기 26:26.content: Then Abimelech went to him from Gerar, and Ahuzzath his friend, and Phicol the captain of his army.

창세기 26:27.content: Isaac said to them, “Why have you come to me, since you hate me, and have sent me away from you?”

창세기 26:28.content: They said, “We saw plainly that Yahweh was with you. We said, ‘Let there now be an oath between us, even between us and you, and let’s make a covenant with you,

창세기 26:29.content: that you will do us no harm, as we have not touched you, and as we have done to you nothing but good, and have sent you away in peace.’ You are now the blessed of Yahweh.”

창세기 26:30.content: He made them a feast, and they ate and drank.

창세기 26:31.content: They rose up some time in the morning, and swore an oath to one another. Isaac sent them away, and they departed from him in peace.

창세기 26:32.content: The same day, Isaac’s servants came, and told him concerning the well which they had dug, and said to him, “We have found water.”

창세기 26:33.content: He called it “Shibah”. Therefore the name of the city is “Beersheba” to this day.

창세기 26:34.content: When Esau was forty years old, he took as wife Judith, the daughter of Beeri the Hittite, and Basemath, the daughter of Elon the Hittite.

창세기 26:35.content: They grieved Isaac’s and Rebekah’s spirits.
